author: mark
text:
Split config ownership: config.json vs project.json

Per SPEC-tracker-state §7, balls config has two owners. The workspace owns how a repo builds and integrates (.balls/config.json, a real never-symlinked file on the code branch); the project owns the shared backlog policy. They were one struct — split them.

New ProjectConfig (.balls/project.json) carries version, id_length, min_bl_version, and the plugins map. It rides the tracker branch, seeded by state_repo and reached through a .balls/project.json symlink into the state checkout. On overlap project.json wins; seed migrates a pre-split config.json's project-owned fields so nothing is lost, and ensure_project_config runs the same migration on a warm checkout discover would never re-seed.

bl plugin enable/disable/policy now write the plugins map to project.json; commit_change publishes it and the plugin config files together on the tracker branch, so the follow-up is bl sync. Adds SPEC §16 conformance tests 5 (config ownership) and 6 (plugin split), both failing against the pre-split single-config.json model.
